description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the registry.gitlab.com/fdroid/ci-images-client:latest Docker image as the default, caches .gradle/wrapper and .gradle/caches, and defines a global after_script that removes the Gradle lock file and plugin‑resolution caches. Define two stages: test and deploy. In the test stage add a job named "connected 22 default armeabi-v7a" that runs a before_script exporting GRADLE_USER_HOME, extracting ANDROID_COMPILE_SDK from app/build.gradle, installing gradle via apt, and then executes a script that runs gradle assembleDebug, derives AVD_SDK, AVD_TAG and AVD_ARCH from the job name, installs the Android emulator, creates an AVD if missing, starts the emulator, waits for it, runs gradle connectedDebugAndroidTest (with a flag for API <25), and on failure uploads logcat.txt, app/build/reports, and APK outputs as artifacts while also running an after_script that echoes a download URL. Also in test add a "test_lint" job that uses the same before_script, runs sed to enable textReport in app/build.gradle, runs gradle build, and shares the same failure artifacts and after_script. Add a "website" job in test that uses the debian:buster-slim image, installs hugo, updates the baseURL in config.yaml with the project namespace and name, builds the site with hugo, verifies that public/es/index.html exists, sets GIT_SUBMODULE_STRATEGY to normal, and runs on all branches except master. In the deploy stage add a "deploy_nightly" job that runs the same before_script, runs only on the master branch, aborts if DEBUG_KEYSTORE is empty, updates the app name to "Checkey Nightly" and the versionCode to the current timestamp via sed, assembles a debug build with gradle, and publishes it with "fdroid nightly -v". Finally, add a "pages" job that also uses debian:buster-slim, runs the same hugo script, publishes the public directory as a GitLab Pages artifact, and runs only on master.
